_comment: MediaRefinery v2 curated model catalog. Schema v2 adds a per-entry `status` field: 'verified' (sha256 + url confirmed), 'unavailable' (no ONNX upstream; entry retained for documentation), or 'pending' (placeholder, code MUST refuse to install). All download URLs are HuggingFace `resolve/main/` paths so we do not depend on the deprecated onnx/models GitHub LFS distribution (sunset 2025-07-01). Updates to this file require code review per threat-model T08.

_curation_findings_2026_05_01.summary: Phase A picks (OpenNSFW2 ONNX + GantMan ONNX) had no upstream ONNX artefact and were dropped. Replaced with AdamCodd/vit-base-nsfw-detector, the only Apache-2.0 NSFW classifier on HuggingFace that ships ONNX directly (multiple quantized variants). The generic ImageNet entry was pivoted from `github.com/onnx/models/raw/<commit>/...` to `huggingface.co/onnxmodelzoo/...` because GitHub LFS for onnx/models was deprecated 2025-07-01; the file bytes are byte-identical (sha256 unchanged) so the pivot is a URL-only change. The five-class multiclass NSFW slot is intentionally dropped for v2.0 — no clean ONNX upstream exists; revisit in v2.1.
verified: mobilenet-v2-imagenet-onnx — onnxmodelzoo HF mirror, sha256 c0c3f76d93fa3fd6580652a45618618a220fced18babf65774ed169de0432ad5, 13964571 bytes
dropped: opennsfw2-onnx-1.0 — bhky/opennsfw2 publishes 20+ releases (latest v0.15.2); none ship ONNX or .h5. Package wraps Keras weights downloaded at runtime.
gantman-mobilenet-v2-onnx — GantMan/nsfw_model 1.1.0/1.2.0 ship Keras .zip files only; no ONNX upstream.

_policy.distribution: no bundled weights; models are downloaded at first run from the urls below with sha256 verification and explicit license acceptance recorded in the audit log
_policy.license_acceptance: the wizard MUST display the full license_text (or the linked license_url) and capture an audit_log entry with actor, model id, sha256, and timestamp before activating the model
_policy.runtime: ONNX Runtime CPU; no Python-side model code execution

models.name: MobileNetV2 ImageNet-1k (ONNX, ONNX Model Zoo via HuggingFace)
models.description: 1000-class ImageNet classifier; lightweight CPU baseline for the universal (non-NSFW) preset. Lets users build categories around generic concepts (dog, beach, document, screenshot, ...) without committing to a sensitive-content model.
models.notes: ImageNet labels are coarse; the dashboard maps user-defined categories to one or more ImageNet labels via the existing classifier-profile YAML schema. URL pivoted from the (now-deprecated) onnx/models GitHub LFS to the HuggingFace onnxmodelzoo mirror; bytes are identical (sha256 unchanged).

models.name: AdamCodd ViT-base NSFW detector (INT8 quantized ONNX)
models.description: Binary safe/NSFW image classifier based on Vision Transformer (ViT-base/224); INT8-quantized ONNX export. Default classifier for the sensitive-media preset. Supersedes the dropped OpenNSFW2 and GantMan entries which had no ONNX upstream.
models.notes: ViT preprocessing: bilinear resize to 224x224, channel-first NCHW, normalised to [-1, 1] (mean 0.5 / std 0.5 per channel). Threshold of 0.85 mirrors the conservative posture of the v1 sensitive-media preset; deployments should re-tune via the dashboard 'preview on N samples' button before enabling live actions.

models.name: AdamCodd ViT-base NSFW detector (Q4+FP16 ONNX, low-footprint)
models.description: Same model as `adamcodd-vit-nsfw-quantized` with Q4 + FP16 quantisation (~50 MB). Use on low-resource deployments where the 88 MB INT8 variant is too large; expect a small accuracy drop versus the INT8 export.
models.notes: Same preprocessing and class semantics as the INT8 variant. Tag this as the recommended download for low-RAM Docker hosts (<2 GB available).
